It is believed to have been a precursor to the {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun}} and highlights the connections between the local female leader buried here and the central {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The kofun site was designated as a Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture in 2004, and its artifacts were designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kobe City in 2009. The Kofun is currently closed to public access. == Excavation History == * '''1927''': Early excavation by Nobuo Nao, revealing cylindrical clay haniwa.
* '''1968''': Survey conducted by the Hyōgo Prefecture Board of Education.
* '''1986-1993''': A series of excavation investigations by the Kobe City Board of Education.
* '''2004''': Designated as a Historic Site by Hyōgo Prefecture.
* '''2009''': Excavated artifacts designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kobe City. == Kofun == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: - Total length: 56 meters - Rear circular section: Diameter 29-32 meters - Front rectangular section: Length 28-29 meters, Width 16.5 meters - Neck width: 14.6 meters The tumulus closely resembles the {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (Sakurai, Nara Prefecture), albeit at approximately one-quarter of its scale. This similarity suggests a potential marital or familial connection between the buried female leader here and the male leader of the Mesuriyama Kofun. == Burial Facilities == The mound contains two burial chambers: * '''1st chamber''': Located in the rear circular mound, comprising a clay burial chamber with a split-log style wooden coffin made from Japanese umbrella pine. The coffin’s interior surface was coated with bengara (red iron oxide), and mercury was detected on the head and chest sections, indicating its high status. * '''2nd chamber''': Located in the front section, also containing a split-log style wooden coffin but without accompanying grave goods. Five cylindrical clay coffin burials were also found surrounding the mound. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the 1st chamber include: * '''Bronze mirror''': One "kakumon-taishinju" mirror (a decorative design with deities and beasts). * '''Stone ornaments''': Including jade magatama, glass beads, and more. * '''Iron tools''': Such as swords and knives. The artifacts exhibit sophisticated craftsmanship, linking them to similar items found at contemporaneous burial sites such as the Mesuriyama Kofun and Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites === - Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on March 9, 2004.{{Cite web|title=Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites List|url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf|accessdate=2024-01-01}} === Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties === - Excavated artifacts from the Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on February 24, 2009.{{Cite web|title=Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/a21651/kanko/bunka/bunkazai/estate/bunkazai/shishite-koko.html|accessdate=2024-01-01}} == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja|神戸市埋蔵文化資料センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Houses the excavated artifacts. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja|池田茶壽山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|version=99995376|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q97173015}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The Kofun, shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is a designated Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the City of Kobe. == Overview == The Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun is located atop Yakushiyama Hill along the right bank of the Ikawa River in southern Hyōgo Prefecture. Excavations led by {{ill|Nobuo Nao|ja|直良信夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobuo Nao|1=Nobuo Naora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581833}} in the early Shōwa period revealed clay cylindrical haniwa. The tomb dates to the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, marking it as the first keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Akashi region. It is believed to have been a precursor to the {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun}} and highlights the connections between the local female leader buried here and the central {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. * '''1968''': Survey conducted by the Hyōgo Prefecture Board of Education.
* '''1986-1993''': A series of excavation investigations by the Kobe City Board of Education.
* '''2004''': Designated as a Historic Site by Hyōgo Prefecture.
* '''2009''': Excavated artifacts designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kobe City. == Kofun == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: - Total length: 56 meters - Rear circular section: Diameter 29-32 meters - Front rectangular section: Length 28-29 meters, Width 16.5 meters - Neck width: 14.6 meters The tumulus closely resembles the {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (Sakurai, Nara Prefecture), albeit at approximately one-quarter of its scale. [[co:commons:Category:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun]][[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun]][[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun]][[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun, enkei.jpg|location={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67497222222222|N|135.00647222222221|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun''' (白水瓢塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Kobe|ja|西区 (Kobe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The Kofun, shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is a designated Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the City of Kobe. == Overview == The Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun is located atop Yakushiyama Hill along the right bank of the Ikawa River in southern Hyōgo Prefecture. Excavations led by {{ill|Nobuo Nao|ja|直良信夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobuo Nao|1=Nobuo Naora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581833}} in the early Shōwa period revealed clay cylindrical haniwa. Further excavation investigations were conducted starting in 1986. The Kofun has a unique "mirrored-handle" shape, with the front section facing west. The Kofun is constructed in two tiers and lacks features such as fukiishi (paving stones) or a surrounding moat. Two burial chambers are present: a clay burial chamber (1st chamber) in the rear circular mound and a split-log style wooden coffin (2nd chamber) in the front section. The mound also contains five surrounding clay cylindrical coffin burials. The tomb dates to the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, marking it as the first keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Akashi region. It is believed to have been a precursor to the {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} and highlights the connections between the local female leader buried here and the central {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The kofun site was designated as a Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture in 2004, and its artifacts were designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kobe City in 2009. The Kofun is currently closed to public access. == Excavation History == * '''1927''': Early excavation by Nobuo Nao, revealing cylindrical clay haniwa.
* '''1968''': Survey conducted by the Hyōgo Prefecture Board of Education.
* '''1986-1993''': A series of excavation investigations by the Kobe City Board of Education.
* '''2004''': Designated as a Historic Site by Hyōgo Prefecture.
* '''2009''': Excavated artifacts designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kobe City. == Kofun == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: - Total length: 56 meters - Rear circular section: Diameter 29-32 meters - Front rectangular section: Length 28-29 meters, Width 16.5 meters - Neck width: 14.6 meters The tumulus closely resembles the {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (Sakurai, Nara Prefecture), albeit at approximately one-quarter of its scale. This similarity suggests a potential marital or familial connection between the buried female leader here and the male leader of the Mesuriyama Kofun. == Burial Facilities == The mound contains two burial chambers: * '''1st chamber''': Located in the rear circular mound, comprising a clay burial chamber with a split-log style wooden coffin made from Japanese umbrella pine. The coffin’s interior surface was coated with bengara (red iron oxide), and mercury was detected on the head and chest sections, indicating its high status. * '''2nd chamber''': Located in the front section, also containing a split-log style wooden coffin but without accompanying grave goods. Five cylindrical clay coffin burials were also found surrounding the mound. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the 1st chamber include: * '''Bronze mirror''': One "kakumon-taishinju" mirror (a decorative design with deities and beasts). * '''Stone ornaments''': Including jade magatama, glass beads, and more. * '''Iron tools''': Such as swords and knives. The artifacts exhibit sophisticated craftsmanship, linking them to similar items found at contemporaneous burial sites such as the Mesuriyama Kofun and Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites === - Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on March 9, 2004.{{Cite web|title=Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites List|url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf|accessdate=2024-01-01}} === Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties === - Excavated artifacts from the Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on February 24, 2009.{{Cite web|title=Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/a21651/kanko/bunka/bunkazai/estate/bunkazai/shishite-koko.html|accessdate=2024-01-01}} == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja|神戸市埋蔵文化資料センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Houses the excavated artifacts. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} * {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja|池田茶壽山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|version=99995376|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q97173015}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

The artifacts exhibit sophisticated craftsmanship, linking them to similar items found at contemporaneous burial sites such as the Mesuriyama Kofun and Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites === - Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on March 9, 2004.{{Cite web|title=Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites List|url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf|accessdate=2024-01-01}} === Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties === - Excavated artifacts from the Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on February 24, 2009.{{Cite web|title=Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/a21651/kanko/bunka/bunkazai/estate/bunkazai/shishite-koko.html|accessdate=2024-01-01}} == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja|神戸市埋蔵文化資料センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Houses the excavated artifacts. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} * {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja|池田茶壽山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|version=99995376|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q97173015}} [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun]][[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun]][[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] [[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun, enkei.jpg|location={{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320|1=Kobe}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67497222222222|N|135.00647222222221|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun''' (白水瓢塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Kobe|ja|西区 (Kobe)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|WD=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. The Kofun, shaped as a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is a designated Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture, and its excavated artifacts have been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by the City of Kobe. == Overview == The Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun is located atop Yakushiyama Hill along the right bank of the Ikawa River in southern Hyōgo Prefecture. Excavations led by {{ill|Nobuo Nao|ja|直良信夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobuo Nao|1=Nobuo Naora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581833}} in the early Shōwa period revealed clay cylindrical haniwa. Further excavation investigations were conducted starting in 1986. The Kofun has a unique "mirrored-handle" shape, with the front section facing west. The Kofun is constructed in two tiers and lacks features such as fukiishi (paving stones) or a surrounding moat. Two burial chambers are present: a clay burial chamber (1st chamber) in the rear circular mound and a split-log style wooden coffin (2nd chamber) in the front section. The mound also contains five surrounding clay cylindrical coffin burials. The tomb dates to the early 4th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, marking it as the first keyhole-shaped tumulus in the Akashi region. It is believed to have been a precursor to the {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} and highlights the connections between the local female leader buried here and the central {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The kofun site was designated as a Historic Site of Hyōgo Prefecture in 2004, and its artifacts were designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kobe City in 2009. The Kofun is currently closed to public access. == Excavation History == * '''1927''': Early excavation by Nobuo Nao, revealing cylindrical clay haniwa.
* '''1968''': Survey conducted by the Hyōgo Prefecture Board of Education.
* '''1986-1993''': A series of excavation investigations by the Kobe City Board of Education.
* '''2004''': Designated as a Historic Site by Hyōgo Prefecture.
* '''2009''': Excavated artifacts designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kobe City. == Kofun == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: - Total length: 56 meters - Rear circular section: Diameter 29-32 meters - Front rectangular section: Length 28-29 meters, Width 16.5 meters - Neck width: 14.6 meters The tumulus closely resembles the {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} (Sakurai, Nara Prefecture), albeit at approximately one-quarter of its scale. This similarity suggests a potential marital or familial connection between the buried female leader here and the male leader of the Mesuriyama Kofun. == Burial Facilities == The mound contains two burial chambers: * '''1st chamber''': Located in the rear circular mound, comprising a clay burial chamber with a split-log style wooden coffin made from Japanese umbrella pine. The coffin’s interior surface was coated with bengara (red iron oxide), and mercury was detected on the head and chest sections, indicating its high status. * '''2nd chamber''': Located in the front section, also containing a split-log style wooden coffin but without accompanying grave goods. Five cylindrical clay coffin burials were also found surrounding the mound. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the 1st chamber include: * '''Bronze mirror''': One "kakumon-taishinju" mirror (a decorative design with deities and beasts). * '''Stone ornaments''': Including jade magatama, glass beads, and more. * '''Iron tools''': Such as swords and knives. The artifacts exhibit sophisticated craftsmanship, linking them to similar items found at contemporaneous burial sites such as the Mesuriyama Kofun and Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites === - Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on March 9, 2004.{{Cite web|title=Hyōgo Prefectural Historic Sites List|url=http://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/gyouseisituhp/top/23kenshitei.pdf|accessdate=2024-01-01}} === Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties === - Excavated artifacts from the Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun: Designated on February 24, 2009.{{Cite web|title=Kobe City Tangible Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/a21651/kanko/bunka/bunkazai/estate/bunkazai/shishite-koko.html|accessdate=2024-01-01}} == Related Facilities == - {{ill|Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja|神戸市埋蔵文化資料センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe City Archaeological Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Houses the excavated artifacts. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|en|Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|WD=Q1538541|lt=Goshikizuka Kofun|1=Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun}} * {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun|1=Mesuriyama Kofun}} * {{ill|Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja|池田茶壽山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda Chausuyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{translated page|ja|白水瓢塚古墳|version=99995376|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:白水瓢塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Shiramizu Hisagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q97173015}} [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Kofun of Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
